members.name: Johan
members.about: Seshy started as a side project and became something I genuinely care about — building one bot that handles everything an ERLC server needs, without compromise. I've been coding since I was 12, and combining that with a love for ERLC communities felt like the obvious move. Outside of development I build websites, lose sleep over video games, and spend way too much time on Discord talking to people I've never met in person.

timeline.title: Seshy development begins
timeline.desc: Started building the first version — core session management and ERLC API integration.

timeline.title: Ares Security launched
timeline.desc: Built and shipped the anti-nuke protection system after seeing too many ERLC servers get destroyed with no recourse.

timeline.title: 500+ servers milestone
timeline.desc: Seshy crossed 500 active servers. Kept it free. Always will.
